您是否曾经感觉到有人可能在看着您？您会在脖子后部产生刺痛的感觉，转身看到一个陌生人在房间对面凝视着您。有时似乎我们可以感觉到人的凝视是一种身体感觉。而且，一眼就能知道一个人的很多事情，例如他们的心情，意图和专注。他们的目光是危险的，有趣的还是有吸引力的？他们是直接凝视还是瞥了一眼？如果“眼睛是通向灵魂的窗口”，那么一目了然，所揭示的远远超过我们所知道的。
  最近的研究表明，人类将注视归因于其物理特性。我们创建了默认的心理计划，其中将其他人的视觉注意力计算为从观看者的眼睛发出并指向感兴趣对象的有力光束。这些心理计划使我们能够采取认知捷径来快速有效地处理人们的视觉注意力。
  注视是沟通的一种基本形式，可以协调活动并传达社交动态，而无需手势或说话。它需要对他人注视背后的含义进行快速解释，但是这种解释速度的权衡是对凝视的错误理解，因为凝视是可以在我们环境中移动事物的事物。这些研究表明，这种解释是潜意识的，自动的，甚至在那些有意识地否认视觉施加任何力量的人中也会发生。
  您可能希望这样的错误解释将是有害的。实际上，尽管几乎没有不良后果，但这些发现可能是凝视的外在力量和力量的丰富多样的文化基础。实验的结果表明了古老的人类观念，将凝视与物理特性联系起来。这个概念与希腊人一样古老，被称为“超越”视觉理论。准入字面意思是“发出”，而准入理论则认为视觉是眼睛发出的力量。这是对儿童普遍存在的视觉的直观理解，并在许多成年人中持续存在。相反，现代视觉理论被称为“介绍性”，其基础是视觉是光线进入眼睛的结果。
  通过一项研究中的一系列巧妙简单的实验，研究人员发现受试者将目光与一种物理力量联系在一起。受试者观看了一个计算机显示器，该显示器具有一个管的图像，大约与纸巾卷末端的大小大致垂直，直立在桌子上。桌子的一端是一张凝视着管子的脸的照片（研究人员称其为“头像”凯文）。指示对象使用键盘上的特定键将试管向凯文的图像倾斜，直到他们感到试管已达到其倾翻的临界角度为止。受试者报告的临界角取决于Kevin是否被蒙住眼睛。如果认为Kevin凝视着管子，则临界角大于当Kevin蒙住眼睛时的临界角，这表明他的凝视给管子施加了一些力，必须克服该力才能使管子掉落。
  同样，在第二个实验中，向受试者展示了凯文（Kevin）的图像，他们注视着管子或朝相反的方向注视，并要求他们在倾倒之前报告管子的临界角。再次，该角度取决于凯文的视线，与凯文转身时相比，凯文直视管子时的角度要大得多。最后，在第三个实验中，受试者被告知凯文要么直接看管子，要么聚焦在桌子另一端的墙上。再一次，如果受试者认为凯文凝视着管子而不是墙壁，那么临界倾斜角会更大。
  事先筛选了参加本研究的参与者是否相信超自然信念，而排除了表达这种信念的人们。因此，值得注意的是，所有剩余的参与者都基于凝视而感受到了一种力量，即使他们否认了从眼睛发出的这种力量的任何信念。这项研究中出现的是人类用来快速处理凝视的一种隐性，无法识别的认知捷径，但是这使我们理解它是影响世界物体的事物。 
为了验证这一理论，研究人员采用大脑成像方法来证明注视感知可以激活与运动相关的大脑区域。在这种情况下，向对象显示运动点的图像或凝视树的脸部图像。使用功能磁共振成像（fMRI）测量大脑活动，该功能通过测量局部大脑耗氧量来检测大脑活动。盯着树看时，涉及处理视觉运动的大脑区域（右侧中颞皮层区域）和理解他人的思想和意图（右侧颞顶叶交界）涉及处理面部的凝视。但是，就像蒙着眼睛的凯文一样，当这些研究中的脸被蒙住眼睛时，这些功能磁共振成像信号也会停止。在这里，即使没有运动发生，大脑也会将视线作为运动进行处理，这再次显示出对现实的极大误解。
  整个世纪以来，对凝视力量的信仰出现在故事​​和神话中。美杜莎的目光使人们陷入僵局。长者普林尼（Pliny）所描述的双足动物，以及更为著名的蛇怪，一眼就能杀死。在莎士比亚的维纳斯和阿多尼斯，维纳斯抱怨阿多尼斯瞥了一眼所造成的痛苦：“那双大眼睛向我扑来，灼伤了我。”在约翰·多恩（John Donne）的《迷魂药》中，恋人的目光交织在一起，将它们束缚在一起，就好像它们是他们的双手一样。当然，如果不提到绝地大师或超人，就不会有完整的凝视文化参考清单。
  注视是社交互动的重要元素。它揭示了一个人将注意力集中在什么地方，而当指向我们时，它可能会产生强烈的情感效果。注视可以在社会组织中发挥作用，直接注视表明了社会主导地位，而注视厌恶则表明了被动性。眼神接触会引起机敏和身体意识，而对眼神接触漠不关心或厌恶则可能表示情绪或神经系统疾病。当我们将目光对准某物或某人时，其他注意到下意识的人也以相同的方式将其目光对准。我们可以利用这种趋势故意影响他人的视线。魔术师利用重定向视线和注意力的能力来增强手掌的技巧。视觉艺术家可以操纵艺术品的属性（例如亮度），以将视觉凝视指向绘画的特定特征。在舞蹈中，凝视可以用来在舞台上的角色之间传达力量动态，而音乐家则依靠凝视作为一种重要的交流手段，在凝视乐队和合唱团的表演时，利用凝视来帮助暗示和同步。
  凝视是一种沟通方式，在很多方面，潜意识，快速，充满活力地影响着我们，以至于一位研究人员将凝视的效果描述为“旺盛的”。虽然魔术师可能知道如何操纵注视以增强幻觉，但将注视幻视作为一种物理力量已经足够了。
note_en:
Have you ever sensed that someone might be watching you? You get a prickly feeling at the back of your neck and turn to see a stranger staring at you across the room. It sometimes seems that we can feel a person’s gaze as a physical sensation. And, from a single glance, we can tell a lot about a person, such as their moods, intentions and focus. Is their gaze dangerous, interesting or attractive? Do they stare directly or glance to the side? If “eyes are the window into the soul,” then a glance reveals far more than we know.
  Recent  studies demonstrate that humans attribute gaze with physical properties. We create tacit mental schemes in which the visual attention of others is computed as a forceful beam emitted from the viewer’s eye and directed at the object of interest. These mental schemes allow us to take cognitive shortcuts to process peoples’ visual attention quickly and efficiently.
  Gaze is an elemental form of communication that can coordinate activities and convey social dynamics without a gesture or spoken word. It requires a rapid interpretation of the meaning behind another’s gaze, but the trade-off for the speed of that interpretation is the mistaken understanding of gaze as something that can move things in our environment. These studies show that this interpretation is subconscious and automatic, and that it occurs even in those who would consciously deny that vision exerts any force.
  You might expect that such an erroneous interpretation would be detrimental. In fact, while there seem to be few if any adverse consequences these findings may underlie rich and diverse cultural references to the outward force and power of the gaze. The results of the experiment demonstrate an ancient human idea linking gaze with physical properties. This notion, as old as the  Greeks, is known as the “extramission” theory of vision. Extramission literally means “sending out,” and the extramission theory is the belief that vision is a force emitted from the eye. It is an intuitive understanding of vision common among children that persists among many adults. In contrast, the modern visual theory is called “intromission,” and is based on the notion that vision results from light entering the eyes.
  Using a series of ingeniously simple experiments in one study, researchers found that subjects associate gaze with a physical force. Subjects viewed a computer display that had an image of a tube, roughly the size of the end of the paper towel roll, standing vertically on a table. At one end of the table was an image of a face gazing at the tube (researchers dubbed the face avatar Kevin). Subjects were instructed to tilt the tube towards Kevin’s image using specific keys on a keyboard until they felt the tube had reached the critical angle at which it would tip over . The critical angle reported by subjects depended upon whether Kevin was blindfolded. If Kevin was perceived as gazing at the tube, the critical angle was greater than when Kevin was blindfolded, suggesting that his gaze was impressing some force upon the tube that needed to be overcome for the tube to fall.
  Likewise, in a second experiment, subjects were presented with the image of Kevin either gazing at the tube or gazing away in the opposite direction and asked to report the critical angle of the tube before toppling. Once again, the angle depended on Kevin’s perceived gaze and was much greater when Kevin gazed straight at the tube compared to when Kevin was turned away. Finally, in a third experiment subjects were told that Kevin was either looking directly at the tube or focused beyond the tube at a wall at the other end of the table. Once again, the critical tilt angle was greater if subjects thought Kevin was gazing at the tube rather than the wall.
  Participants in this study were screened for belief in extramission beforehand and those who expressed such a belief were excluded. So, it is remarkable that all remaining participants intuited a force based upon gaze, even while they disavowed any belief in such a force emanating from the eye. What has emerged in this study is an implicit, unrecognized cognitive shortcut employed by humans to rapidly process gaze, but which leads us to comprehend it as something that affects objects in the world.
  To test this theory, researchers  employed brain imaging methods to demonstrate that gaze perception activates brain regions associated with motion. In this case, subjects were presented images of moving dots or an image of a face gazing at a tree. Brain activity was measured using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), which detects brain activity by measuring local brain oxygen consumption. Areas of the brain involved in processing visual motion (the right middle temporal cortical areas) and in understanding the thoughts and intentions of others (the right temporal parietal junction) were involved in processing the face’s gaze when staring at the tree. However, just as with the blindfolded Kevin, these fMRI signals halted when the face in these studies was blindfolded. Here, the brain processes the gaze as movement even when no movement occurs, again showing an extraordinary misapprehension of reality.
  Belief in the power of gaze appears in stories and myths throughout the centuries.  Medusa turned people to stone with her gaze. The  catoblepas and, more famously, the  basilisk, both described by Pliny the Elder, could kill with the single glance. In  Shakespeare’s  Venus and Adonis, Venus complains of the pain caused by Adonis’ glance: “Thine eye darts forth the fire that burneth me.” While in John Donne’s  The   Ecstasy , the glances of the lovers intertwine and bind them as if they were their clasped hands. And, of course, no list of cultural references to gaze would be complete without mention of the Jedi master or Superman.
  Gaze is a powerful element of social interaction. It reveals where a person is focusing their attention, and, when directed at us, it can have a strong emotional effect. Gaze can play a role in social organization, with a direct gaze demonstrating social  dominance and gaze aversion indicating passivity. Eye contact can elicit alertness and bodily  awareness, while  indifference or aversion to eye contact can signal emotional or  neurological disorders. When we direct our gaze at something or someone, others who notice subconsciously direct their gaze in the same manner. We can take advantage of this tendency to deliberately influence the gaze of others.  Magicians take advantage of the ability to  redirect gaze and attention to enhance their sleight of hand. Visual  artists can manipulate attributes of a work of art such as luminosity in order to direct visual gaze to specific features of a painting. In  dance, gaze can be used to convey the power dynamics between the characters on stage, while  musicians rely upon gaze as an essential means of communication, using it to help in cuing and synchronization during the performances of orchestras and choirs.
  Gaze is a means of communication that impacts us in many ways, subconsciously and quickly, so quickly and energetically that one investigator described the effects of gaze as “ exuberant.” And while magicians may know how to manipulate gaze to enhance their illusions, the illusion of gaze as a physical force is magic enough.
title: 当我们的目光是肉体力量时
title_en: When Our Gaze Is a Physical Force

CoStar是一家商业房地产数据和分析提供商，还经营着Apartments.com和ApartmentFinder.com等挂牌网站，该公司于2月同意以5.88亿美元的价格收购RentPath。全现金交易是在RentPath表示将申请第11章破产保护后提出的。据《华尔街日报》报道，RentPath已经聘请了财务顾问来重组超过6.5亿美元的债务。
 但本月初，联邦贸易委员会在联邦法院批准了一项反托拉斯诉讼，以阻止收购。 FTC竞争局副局长丹尼尔·弗朗西斯（Daniel Francis）在一份声明中表示，“此次收购将消除对租房者和物业管理者均有利的价格和质量竞争，”因为CoStar和RentPath的竞争将广告费保留在其平台上，其中包括一些最受欢迎的列表网站中，排名较低。
  RentPath在今天的公告中表示，其第11章计划仍得到贷方的支持，包括具有“在类似情况下成功投资企业的良好记录”的另类资产管理公司。
  FTC的诉讼和RentPath决定退出收购协议之际，世界各地越来越多的国家正在打击技术整合。尽管美国在反托拉斯行动方面落后于其他政府，但这种情况正在逐渐改变，亚马逊，谷歌和Facebook受到了更多的立法审查，最近有46个州对Facebook提起诉讼，指控其“非法”收购竞争对手。增加其市场力量。
 RentPath / CoStar交易的命运也可能预示着对美国proptech公司进行更多的反托拉斯审查。 CoStar在过去十年中通过收购扩大了业务，目前正在进行其他交易，包括上市网站HomeSnap（上个月已通过FTC审查），以及据报道对房地产分析公司CoreLogic的竞标。 CoStar和RentPath的竞争对手Zillow还因通过一系列收购而建立了自己的业务，其中包括2014年以35亿美元收购Trulia。
note_en:
CoStar, a commercial real estate data and analytics provider that also operates listing sites like Apartments.com and ApartmentFinder.com,  agreed in February to buy RentPath for $588 million. The all-cash deal came after  RentPath   said it would file for chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. RentPath had already hired financial advisors to restructure more than $650 million in debt,  reported the Wall Street Journal.
 But earlier this month, the Federal Trade Commission authorized an antitrust lawsuit in federal court to block the acquisition. Daniel Francis, deputy director of the FTC’s Bureau of Competition,  said in a statement that “the acquisition will eliminate price and quality competition that benefits both renters and property managers,” because CoStar and RentPath’s rivalry kept advertising rates on their platform, which include some of the most popular listing sites, low.
  In its announcement today, RentPath said its chapter 11 plan remains backed by lenders, including alternative asset management firms with “strong track records of successfully investing in businesses under similar circumstances.”
  The FTC’s lawsuit and RentPath’s decision to back out of the acquisition agreement comes as  more countries around the world are cracking down on tech consolidation. While the United States has trailed behind other governments in terms of antitrust actions, that is gradually changing, with Amazon, Google and Facebook coming under more legislative scrutiny, and the  recent lawsuit filed by 46 states against Facebook alleging that it bought competitors “illegally” to increase its market power.
 The fate of the RentPath/CoStar deal may foreshadow more antitrust scrutiny for proptech companies in the United States, too. CoStar built out its business over the past decade through acquisitions and has other deals currently in the works, including listings site HomeSnap, which  passed FTC review last month, and a  reported bid for property analytics company CoreLogic. CoStar and RentPath competitor Zillow is also known for  building its business through a series of acquisitions, including Trulia for $3.5 billion in 2014.
title: FTC反托拉斯诉讼后，RentPath放弃与CoStar的收购交易
title_en: RentPath drops acquisition deal with CoStar after FTC antitrust lawsuit

该公司在招聘工作中表示，对Amazon Future Engineer的初步研究旨在将计算机科学教育带给服务水平低下和代表性不足的儿童和年轻人。印度目前正在``进行中''，选择的候选人将承担任务与当地的公益组织和政府官员合作。
 该公司在帖子中表示，计划在2021年在印度启动该计划。“童年到职业”计划目前在美国运行，该公司为5,000多家学校和550,000名学生提供计算机科学课程，在本周早些时候的新闻稿中说。
  该公司在描述中说：“亚马逊印度公司特别注重为服务水平低下和贫困的社区的儿童和年轻人提供装备，以为自己创造更好的未来。”该公司没有立即回应置评请求。
 到目前为止，这家美国电子商务巨头已经在印度投资了超过65亿美元，多年来一直在探索印度的教育空间。去年，它推出了JEE Ready，该应用程序旨在帮助准备进入印度著名技术学院的学生。自此，JEE Ready被更名为Amazon Academy，提供免费的在线课程，并在模拟测试中分析学生的表现。
  亚马逊并不是唯一关注印度不断增长的教育市场的美国公司，印度有超过2.6亿儿童上学，而且很多人都认为教育是经济发展和改善生活的关键。
 今年早些时候，Facebook与监督印度私立和公立学校教育的政府机构中央中学教育委员会（CBSE）合作，推出了有关数字安全和在线幸福感以及增强现实的认证课程和教育者。
 Facebook今年还投资了Unacademy，这是一家位于班加罗尔的初创公司，提供在线学习课程。谷歌今年投资了印度教育科技初创公司Cuemath，它还与CBSE合作，在印度培训了超过100万名教师，并提供了一系列免费工具，例如G Suite for Education，Google Classroom和YouTube，以帮助将教育体验数字化在全国。 
微软还与包括国家技能开发公司和Nasscom在内的几个印度政府和行业机构合作，帮助超过100万人提高了自己的技能。
note_en:
In a job recruitment post, the company said that initial research for  Amazon Future Engineer, through which it aims to bring computer science education to underserved and underrepresented children and young adults, in India is “currently underway” and the chosen candidate would be tasked with working with local nonprofits and government officials.
 The company  said in the post that it plans to launch the program in India in 2021. The childhood-to-career program is currently operational in the United States, where the company serves more than 5,000 schools and 550,000 students with computer science coursework, it  said in a press release earlier this week.
  “Amazon India has a specific focus on equipping children and young adults from underserved and underprivileged communities to build better futures for themselves,” the company said in the description. The company did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
 The American e-commerce giant, which has  invested more than $6.5 billion in India so far, has been exploring the education space in the country for a few years. Last year, it launched JEE Ready, an app aimed at helping students who are preparing for entry into India’s prestigious technology institutes. JEE Ready, which has since been rebranded as  Amazon Academy, offers free online classes and analyzes students’ performance in mock tests.
  Amazon isn’t the only American firm that is paying attention to India’s growing education market, where more than 260 million children go to school and much of the population sees education as a key to economic progress and a better life.
 Earlier this year,  Facebook    partnered with the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), a government body that oversees education in private and public schools in India, to launch a certified curriculum on digital safety and online well-being, and augmented reality for students and educators.
 Facebook this year also  invested in Unacademy, a Bangalore-based startup that offers online learning classes. Google, which  invested in Indian edtech startup Cuemath this year, has  also partnered with CBSE to train more than 1 million teachers in India and offer a range of free tools such as G Suite for Education, Google Classroom and YouTube to help digitize the education experience in the nation.
 Microsoft   has also collaborated with several Indian government and industry bodies including  National Skill Development Corporation, and  Nasscom to help more than 1 million people upskill themselves.
title: 亚马逊计划在印度启动其计算机科学教育计划
title_en: Amazon eyes launching its computer science education program in India

note:
简而言之：不论好坏，另一项服务正在加入Google墓地。作为Google长期关闭服务中的最新一次，Cloud Print将在短短几天内终止，这意味着ChromeOS客户或其他人将无法再使用它。
  大多数互联网用户可能从未一次使用过Cloud Print，它主要是为几年前访问受限或无法访问传统打印机的ChromeOS客户设计的。
 但是，由于ChromeOS拥有对打印设备的广泛支持，因此Cloud Print实际上已经过时了。它仍然具有一些独特的优势，例如与朋友共享打印机的能力，但是在大多数情况下，Google没有理由保留它。
  换句话说，在最坏的情况下，Cloud Print的终止会对某些人造成不便。对于其他人，甚至是Chrome操作系统的用户，您只需要记住将默认打印机切换到其他设备上就很容易了。
 Cloud Print关闭的确切日期是2020年12月31日，距离现在只有两天了。如果您出于某种原因而喜欢使用该服务，请确保在除夕之前完成所有打印任务。
 如果您不确定如何退出Cloud Print，则Google会提供完整的＆migration＆＃34;指导帮助您。
note_en:
In brief: Another service is joining the Google graveyard, for better or worse. As the latest in a long series of Google service shutdowns, Cloud Print will be terminated in just a few short days, meaning it will no longer be accessible for ChromeOS customers or others.
  Most internet users have probably never used  Cloud Print a single time -- it was primarily designed for ChromeOS customers who had limited or no access to traditional printers years ago.
 However, now that ChromeOS boasts much broader support for printing devices, Cloud Print has effectively become obsolete. It still has a few unique advantages, such as the ability to share your printers with friends, but for the most part, there's no reason for Google to keep it around.
  In other words, at worst, Cloud Print's termination will be a mild inconvenience to some. For others, even ChromeOS users, you'll simply need to remember to switch your default printer to a different device -- easy enough.
 The exact date of Cloud Print's shutdown is December 31, 2020, which is just two days away. If you loved using the service for one reason or another, be sure to get all your printing tasks done before New Year's Eve.
 If you're unsure how to switch away from Cloud Print, Google has a full  "migration" guide to help you out.
title: Google的“云打印”服务即将关闭
title_en: Google's 'Cloud Print' Service is Shutting Down Soon

像其他所有人一样，图书出版商将很高兴看到2020年年底。但是对于许多出版商来说，这一年带来了一些积极的消息，令人惊讶的是，这是令人欣喜的：生意很好。
 如此多的人呆在家里，从音乐会到电影的活动都超出了限制，人们一直在阅读很多书，或者至少买了很多书。根据NPD BookScan的数据，今年到目前为止，单位印刷销售额增长了近8％。占市场份额较小的电子书和有声书也有所增加。
 企鹅兰登书屋美国公司首席执行官马德琳·麦金托什（Madeline McIntosh）谈到该行业时说：“我希望在年底查看最终数字时，这将是很长一段时间里最好的一年。 ”
 当美国在3月猛然关门时，图书销量急剧下降，但这种下降并没有持续。尽管该行业的某些部门仍在挣扎，例如书店和教育出版商，但出版业高管表示，需求在6月左右回升。
 其中许多销售都流向了亚马逊，但大型商店，尤其是Target，也做得很好。作为出售杂货之类的必不可少的企业，他们被允许通过封锁保持开放。 ReaderLink的首席执行官Dennis Abboud是沃尔玛，Target和Costco等主要连锁店的书籍发行商，他说，他的公司的在线销售额比去年增长了三倍。
 阿布德说：“这实际上是两个城市的故事。” “年初是超级软，而年底是超级强。”
 根据美国出版商协会的数据，尽管今年通勤人数下降了，但有声读物的收入比2019年同期增长了17％以上，而电子书的销量在过去几年中一直在下降，涨幅超过16％。 
今年的图书销售中有一些特别有力的主题。 5月底乔治·弗洛伊德（George Floyd）被杀之后，黑人生活问题（Black Lives Matter）抗议活动引起了有关种族和反种族主义书籍的热捧。书店在保留诸如Ibram X. Kendi撰写的“如何成为反种族主义者”和Ijeoma Oluo撰写的“所以你想谈论种族”等股票时遇到了麻烦。
 政治书籍，特别是有关特朗普总统的书籍，也表现良好。这对西蒙（Simon＆amp;）来说是特别的福音。舒斯特（Schuster）出版了当年一些最大的总统通缉令，其中包括玛丽·特朗普（Mary L. Trump）的《太多了，永远不够》（The Too Much and Never Enough），第一周就售出了135万本。前总统巴拉克·奥巴马（Barack Obama）的回忆录《应许之地》由皇冠出版，是企鹅兰登书屋的印记，自上个月出版以来，在北美已售出330万本，而且在一些国家也很畅销。如德国，法国，巴西和瑞典。
 但是，在普通兴趣出版市场上的实力已经超越了几个标题和类别。在行业内被称为头版的新书销量不错，但较旧的书名也是如此。
  麦金托什在谈到奥巴马的书和格伦农·道尔的回忆录时说：“绝对有像“应许之地”或“不受驯服”这样的前线成功。 “但是像'非常饥饿的毛毛虫'这样的东西比过去出售了更多的副本。这就是整个市场的飞速发展。”
 出版高管长期以来一直将自己的业务描述为可以抵御衰退。毕竟，与花在书上的时间相比，书相对便宜。 NPD BookScan表示，市场在2008年经济衰退后保持稳定，当失业率在2009年开始达到顶峰并在第二年恢复增长时，仅下跌了4％。然而，这次的挑战感到更加严峻，因为社会距离遥远，仓库受到限制，封锁大量，经济迅速崩溃。
 但是，供应链问题，例如大型印刷公司的产能问题，管理起来很棘手，而且仍在持续进行中，并没有使系统停顿下来。同时，大流行的限制消除了一些竞争。一个人只能观看那么多Netflix，因此没有太多其他选择。
 麦克米伦（Macmillan）首席执行官唐·韦斯伯格（Don Weisberg）说：“对于休闲时间的竞争，这种关系在大流行中已经改变了。” “如何恢复将是未来的有力指标。” 
书本世界的部分地区一直在挣扎。根据BookScan的说法，随着许多教堂和其他礼拜堂的关闭，宗教书籍的销量下降了，旅行类别的印刷量下降了40％以上。 （另一方面，年轻的成年人小说以及有关家庭和园艺的书籍增加了20％以上。）
 具有较大教育部门的出版商也比一般兴趣的出版商走得更艰难。例如，霍顿·米夫林·哈科特（Houghton Mifflin Harcourt）于10月宣布将裁员22％。
 独立书店度过了非常艰难的一年。许多人几个月来都被禁止通行，并争先恐后地将他们的商店变成在线订单的履行中心，这是他们从来没有大规模建立的。一些商店在今年早些时候向美国书商协会报告说，他们的销售额下降了至少40％。但是贸易组织首席执行官艾里森·希尔说，过去几周令人鼓舞。
 她说：“假期期间的赌注很高。” “直到假期完全结束，我们才真正知道每个人的立场。”
  巴恩斯来宝（Noble）在某些领域也很挣扎-首席执行官詹姆斯·达恩特（James Daunt）将其纽约商店的业绩描述为“坦率地说，完全是一场灾难”。但他说，总的来说，事情还没有像预期的那样可怕，并且部分原因是因为裁员和其他削减成本的措施，他预计今年比去年更有利可图。今年有将近5,000名在公司工作不到六个月的员工被解雇，其中大部分是兼职。中央办公室的一百多人也被放走了。
 该公司在城市以外的商店现在表现良好，并且在大流行初期，亚马逊将图书和其他物品的优先级降低，从而可以专注于医疗用品和家庭必需品，其在线业务得到了提振。
 “ Barnes＆amp;贵族得益于这样的事实，即在大流行开始之时，亚马逊只是不知所措，” Daunt先生说。 “人们发现这实际上不是垄断。还有另一位大玩家。我们一直坚持下去。” 
即使对于表现出色的出版商，对书店的担忧，尤其是那些不属于连锁店的书店，也会令他们感到担忧。 读者不太可能在网上购物时发现从未听说过的好书，但书商或员工精选表可能会将他们介绍给装满新书名的手提袋。  独立出版商《七故事出版社》（Seven Stories Press）的创始人和出版商丹·西蒙（Dan Simon）表示：“我们的销售额在奇迹般的2020年将增长。” “但是，当您谈论引入新的声音时，这可能是我们要做的最重要的事情，我认为这将在2020年遭受很多打击。”  他说，无法轻松地在线替换书店中的个人对个人销售。 “那是我们到今年年底的地方，带有问号。”  在Facebook，Twitter和Instagram上关注《纽约时报》，注册我们的新闻通讯或文学日历。 在Book Review播客上聆听我们的声音。
note_en:
Like everybody else, book publishers will be happy to see the end of 2020. But for many of them, the year has brought some positive news, which has been as welcome as it was surprising: Business has been good.
 With so many people stuck at home and activities from concerts to movies off limits, people have been reading a lot — or at least buying a lot of books. Print sales by units are up almost 8 percent so far this year, according to NPD BookScan. E-books and audiobooks, which make up a smaller portion of the market, are up as well.
 “I expect that at the end of the year, when you look at the final numbers,”  Madeline McIntosh, chief executive of Penguin Random House U.S., said of the industry, “it will have been the best year in a very long time.”
 When the United States slammed shut in March, book sales  dropped sharply, but the dip didn’t last. While some parts of the industry have continued to struggle, like  bookstores and educational publishers, publishing executives say that demand came rushing back around June.
 Many of these sales went to Amazon, but  big-box stores, especially Target, also did well. As essential businesses that sold things like groceries, they were allowed to stay open through the lockdowns. Dennis Abboud, chief executive of ReaderLink, a book distributor to major chains like Walmart, Target and Costco, said his company’s online sales nearly quadrupled over last year.
 “It was really a tale of two cities,” Mr. Abboud said. “The beginning of the year was mega soft, and the end of the year was mega strong.”
 Even though the number of people commuting has plummeted this year, audiobook revenue is up more than 17 percent over the same period in 2019, according to the Association of American Publishers, and e-book sales, which had been declining for the past several years, are up more than 16 percent.
 There have been a few particularly powerful themes in book selling this year. The Black Lives Matter protests following the killing of George Floyd at the end of May caused a  rush on books about race and antiracism. Bookstores had trouble  keeping titles in stock like “How to Be an Antiracist,” by  Ibram X. Kendi, and “So You Want to Talk About Race,” by Ijeoma Oluo.
 Political books,  especially about President Trump, have also performed well. That was a particular  boon for Simon & Schuster, which published some of the biggest presidential tell-alls of the year, including Mary L. Trump’s “ Too Much and Never Enough,” which sold more than 1.35 million copies in its first week. Former President  Barack Obama’s memoir, “ A Promised Land,” published by Crown, an imprint of Penguin Random House, has sold more than 3.3 million copies in North America since it was published last month, and it has also been a best seller in countries like Germany, France, Brazil and Sweden.
 But the strength in the general-interest publishing market has gone beyond a few titles and categories. New books, which in industry-speak are called the frontlist, have sold well, but so have older titles.
  “There have been frontlist successes like ‘A Promised Land’ or ‘Untamed,’ absolutely,” Ms. McIntosh said of Mr. Obama’s book and  a memoir by Glennon Doyle. “But things like ‘The Very Hungry Caterpillar’ have sold more copies than we have in the past. It’s just this remarkable lift of the whole market.”
 Publishing executives have long described their business as recession-proof. Books, after all, are relatively cheap for the amount of time you spend with them. NPD BookScan said the market remained stable after the 2008 recession, dipping just 4 percent when unemployment started to peak in 2009 and regaining ground the following year. The challenges this time, however, felt more extreme, with social distancing and restrictions at warehouses, vast lockdowns and a rapid economic meltdown.
 But supply chain problems, like capacity issues at large  printing companies — which have been tricky to manage and are ongoing — have not ground the system to a halt. The restrictions of the pandemic, meanwhile, shaved away some of the competition. A person can watch only so much Netflix, and there weren’t a lot of other options.
 “The competition for leisure time, that equation has changed over the pandemic,” said Don Weisberg, the chief executive of Macmillan. “How that comes back will be a strong indicator of the future.”
 Parts of the book world have struggled. With many churches and other houses of worship closed, the sale of religious books has dropped, according to BookScan, and the travel category has tanked by more than 40 percent in print. (Young adult fiction, on the other hand, and books on home and gardening are up more than 20 percent.)
 Publishers with big educational divisions have also had a more difficult road than general interest publishers. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt, for example, announced in October that it was cutting 22 percent of its work force.
 Independent bookstores have had an  extraordinarily difficult year. Many were closed to foot traffic for months, and scrambled to turn their stores into fulfillment centers for online orders, something they were never built to do on a significant scale. Some stores reported to the American Booksellers Association earlier this year that their sales were down at least 40 percent. But Allison K. Hill, the trade organization’s chief executive, said the last few weeks have been encouraging.
 “The stakes were very high going into the holiday season,” she said. “We won’t really know where everybody stands until the holiday season is completely finished.”
  Barnes & Noble has also struggled in some areas — James Daunt, its chief executive, describes the performance of its New York City stores as “frankly, a total disaster.” But overall, he said, things have not been as dire as expected, and — in part because of job cuts and other cost-cutting measures — he expects this year to be more profitable than last. Nearly 5,000 employees who had worked fewer than six months with the company, most of them part time, were laid off this year. More than 100 people in the central office were also let go.
 The company’s stores outside of cities are now performing well, and its online business got a boost when, early in the pandemic, Amazon  deprioritized books and other items so it could focus on medical supplies and household staples.
 “Barnes & Noble benefited from the fact that Amazon, at the outset of the pandemic, was just overwhelmed,” Mr. Daunt said. “People discovered it actually isn’t a monopoly. There’s another big player out there. We’ve held on to that business.”
 Even for publishers who have done well, concerns about bookstores, particularly those that aren’t part of chains, weigh on them. Readers are less likely to discover a great book they’ve never heard of while shopping online, but a bookseller or staff picks table might introduce them to a tote bag full of new titles.
 “Our sales are way up in 2020, miraculously,” said Dan Simon, the founder and publisher of Seven Stories Press, an independent publisher. “However, when you talk about introducing new voices, which is arguably the most important thing we do, I think that’s suffered in 2020 a lot.”
 The individual person-to-person sales that happen in bookstores can’t be easily replaced online, he said. “That’s where we end the year, with a question mark.”
  Follow New York Times Books on   Facebook ,   Twitter  and   Instagram , sign up for   our newsletter  or   our literary calendar . And listen to us on the   Book Review podcast .
title: 出版商的惊喜结局：2020年，生意蒸蒸日上
title_en: Surprise Ending for Publishers: In 2020, Business Was Good

note:
根据The Verge获得的法案文本，麦康奈尔周二晚提出了一项新法案，将增加的刺激付款与第230节的全部废除联系在一起。该法案出台之际，是直接支付2000美元刺激资金的新势头，以及各党派领导人越来越大的压力来安抚特朗普总统不断增长的要求。
 民主党领导人批评将第230条的废除包括在内是为了破坏刺激性谈判。参议院少数党领袖查克·舒默（Chuck Schumer）（D-NY）在星期二的一份声明中说：“参议员麦康奈尔（McConnell）知道如何使2,000美元的生存检查成为现实，并且他知道如何杀死他们。” “参议院共和党人会和麦康奈尔的愤世嫉俗主义者一起走，还是会迫使他对独立的[法案]进行投票？”
 麦康奈尔（McConnell）要求完全废除第230条之际，就越来越多的关于直接支付水平的谈判陷入混乱之中，直接支付水平被纳入刺激措施中。周日，特朗普总统签署了一项法律，向国会批准了9000亿美元的COVID-19救济和政府支出计划，该计划将向大多数美国人提供600美元的刺激资金。签署该法案后，特朗普在公开声明中敦促国会领导人就直接支付增加至2,000美元进行一次独立投票。
  到星期二早上，许多先前反对增加刺激性检查的共和党人与民主党人一起支持他们。特别是，佐治亚州参议院的现任参议员戴维·珀杜（Sen. David Perdue）和凯利·洛夫勒（Kelly Loeffler）周二宣布，他们将在数天回避问题后投票批准追加付款，这给麦康奈尔带来了困难。
 参议员乔什·霍利（Jo-Hawley）（R-MO）在周二的一条推文中表示，参议院已“投票”通过批准增加的付款。但当天晚些时候，麦康奈尔阻止了一项众议院法案，该法案将向大多数美国人提供2,000美元的刺激性付款，理由是有必要解决更高的付款以及总统特鲁姆p提出的其他问题。
 在周二发表的发言中，参议院多数党领袖麦康奈尔（R-KY）将一些无关的政策措施与增加刺激性检查的未来联系在一起。其中包括《通信规范法》第230条，该法律保护Facebook和Google等高科技公司免受用户对其平台上发布的内容的法律责任。
 麦康奈尔在星期二下午说：“在这一过程中，总统特别强调了他希望国会共同解决的三个具有国家重大意义的问题。” “本周，参议院将开始着手处理这三个优先事项。” 
甚至在该法案出台之前，一些立法者就担心多数党领袖会以第230节为毒丸，注定要毁灭更广泛的一揽子计划。
 参议员克里斯·墨菲（Chris Murphy）（D-CT）周二在参议院发言时说：“这是整个努力瓦解的邀请。”
 近几个月来，特朗普经常将第230节用作谈判工具，以推动其他政策优先事项或阻止不必要的立法。上周，特朗普否决了7400亿美元的《国防授权法案》（NDAA），此前有人抱怨称国防法案中没有废除第230条的规定。参议员伯尼·桑德斯（I-VT）威胁周二阻止参议院投票。如果麦康奈尔不同意对2,000美元的支票进行独立投票，则推翻特朗普的NDAA否决权。
 “大技术一定不能获得第230条的保护！”特朗普周日在一份声明中说。 “必须纠正投票欺诈！”
 20/29/20，美国东部时间下午5:28更新：本文的先前版本在美国东部时间下午2:33发布，标题为“第230节已成为正在进行的刺激性谈判的筹码。”当完整的帐单文本可用时，该文章已更新为当前形式，包括新的标题和时间戳。
note_en:
On Tuesday night, McConnell introduced a new bill tying increased stimulus payments to a full repeal of Section 230, according to  bill text obtained by  The Verge. The bill comes amid new momentum for direct $2000 stimulus payments, and increasing pressure on party leaders to appease President Trump’s escalating demands.
 Democratic party leaders criticized the inclusion of Section 230 repeal as an effort to scuttle stimulus talks. “Senator McConnell knows how to make $2,000 survival checks reality and he knows how to kill them,” Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY) said in a statement Tuesday. “Will Senate Republicans go along with Sen. McConnell’s cynical gambit or will they push him to give a vote on the standalone [bill]?”
 McConnell’s bid for a full repeal of Section 230 comes amid increasingly chaotic negotiating over the level of direct payments to be included as part of stimulus efforts. On Sunday, President Trump signed into law Congress’ $900 billion COVID-19 relief and government spending package that would provide $600 in stimulus payments to most Americans.  In a public statement after signing the bill, Trump urged congressional leaders to hold a standalone vote on increasing direct payments to $2,000.
  By Tuesday morning, many Republicans who previously opposed the increased stimulus checks flipped to support of them alongside Democrats. In particular, both  Georgia Senate runoff incumbents, Sens. David Perdue and Kelly Loeffler (R-GA), announced Tuesday that they would vote to approve the additional payments after days of sidestepping the question, creating a difficult position for McConnell.
 Sen. Josh Hawley (R-MO) said in a tweet Tuesday that the Senate has “got the votes” to approve the increased payments. But later that day, McConnell blocked a House bill that would provide $2,000 in stimulus payments to most Americans, citing the need to tackle higher payments alongside other issues raised by President Trum p.
 In a floor statement made Tuesday, Senate majority leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) linked a handful of unrelated policy measures to the future of the increased stimulus checks; amongst them, Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act, a law that shields tech companies like Facebook and Google from legal liability over the content users post on their platforms.
 “During this process, the president highlighted three additional issues of national significance he would like to see Congress tackle together,” McConnell said Tuesday afternoon. “This week, the Senate will begin a process to bring these three priorities into focus.”
 Even before the bill was introduced, some lawmakers feared the majority leader would use Section 230 as a poison pill to doom the broader package.
 “That is an invitation for this entire effort to fall apart,” Sen. Chris Murphy (D-CT) said on the Senate floor Tuesday.
 In recent months, Trump has often used Section 230 as a negotiating tool to push forward other policy priorities or tie up unwanted legislation. Last week, Trump  vetoed the $740 billion National Defense Authorization Act (or NDAA), after complaints that the defense bill did not include a provision that would repeal Section 230. Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT) threatened Tuesday to block a Senate vote to override Trump’s NDAA veto if McConnell did not agree to open a standalone vote on $2,000 checks.
 “Big Tech must not get protections of Section 230!” Trump said in a statement Sunday. “Voter fraud must be fixed!”
 Updated 12/29/20 at 5:28 PM ET:  A previous version of this article was published at 2:33PM ET under the headline “Section 230 has become a bargaining chip in ongoing stimulus talks.” The article was updated to its current form, including a new headline and timestamp, when full bill text became available.
title: 麦康奈尔完全废除第230条，以推动2,000美元的刺激性支票
title_en: McConnell Ties Full Repeal of Section 230 To Push for $2,000 Stimulus Checks

title: 南华早报

note:
3月4日，我上下班前往Vox Media纽约市总部，这最终是2020年被抛弃的那一年的最后一次。在途中，我进站喝了点咖啡，花了3.89美元买了一瓶变色龙冷饮，但离开时没有交出卡或现金。那是因为我已经在某个电子商务巨头拥有的无现金便利店（亚马逊）进行了最终购买。九个月后，最后一站似乎预示着今年将席卷美国大部分生活的重大变化：我们的购物方式和地点。
 在接下来的几周中，随着Covid-19大流行席卷整个美国，我的家人以及数百万在美国的其他人开始以创纪录的数量依赖亚马逊以及其他购物网站和应用程序。口罩，卫生纸，肥皂和洗手液的需求量很大。但是，食品杂货，餐厅，拼图，打印机甚至哑铃也是如此。
 有时，在美国部分地区，政府强制关闭商店意味着，如果您需要购买不必要的商品，唯一的购买地点是“基本”大型商店或在线商店。结果，亚马逊和其他零售巨头（如塔吉特和沃尔玛）获得了回报，而零售连锁店和出售服装或其他“非必要”商品的小型精品店则被迫关门并拒绝顾客。仅在今年，就有近10,000家美国商店永久关闭。
 回顾2020年的商业世界，我们会回想起这一年，因为在线购物已不再是零售业的未来，而扎根于现在。今年是地方政府强迫我们放弃数周或数月的店内购物的一年，然后当我们有机会在店家重新开业时返回时，无论如何，我们大部分时间还是在网上购物。
 以前避开商店在线购物的人们现在坚持使用它是有原因的：通常比浏览成排的走道来查找所需的东西要方便得多。但是，今年在线购物的加速（否则要花几年时间才能实现）将对数百万美国人的工作方式产生深远的影响。公司权力集中的方式；以及重建当地社区的方式，以应对零售商店链（例如百货商店和他们长期定位的购物中心）的衰落。
  万事达卡的数据显示，到去年年底，只有大约13％的零售购买（不包括汽车和天然气销售）是在线购买的。到2020年底，这个数字约为20％，即每5美元中就有1美元。在正常的近几年中，当电子商务的平均增长率在12％至16％之间时，这种跳跃将需要几年的时间才能实现。但是，到2020年，美国的电子商务销售额将增长30％以上，而且没有回头路可走。
 毫无疑问，亚马逊已成为这一巨大转变的巨大赢家，据eMarketer称，亚马逊今年在美国的零售业务增长了约39％，市场份额增加到美国所有在线零售的39％。这家电子商务巨头发布了创纪录的销售和利润，即使有早期的仓库容量问题和交付延迟，内部劳资纠纷，以及在与Covid-19相关的预防措施上花费了数十亿美元。 
直到假期结束，它都展现出了无与伦比的优势。尽管许多其他零售商都停止了提前一两个星期圣诞节前准时交货的承诺，但亚马逊仍保证在12月23日之前完成当天和第二天的某些在线订单的交付，部分原因是其在整个仓库的巨大仓库覆盖率美国并继续扩大自己的送货网络。
 但是亚马逊并不孤单。沃尔玛（Walmart）和塔吉特（Target）也经历了很多年，在在线零售中取得了适度的市场份额增长。他们成功的部分原因在于，在大流行的最初几个月中，在网上销售杂货和其他按需商品，以及已经存在的在线订购路边和店内取货产品。
   小型企业还必须将其销售在线上进行。这种转变的规模体现在迎合不愿依赖亚马逊的中小型商家的技术平台的成功。 Shopify向中小型商家销售电子商务软件工具，在今年的前九个月中，其收入同比增长了近100％。
 这家总部位于加拿大的软件巨人得益于小型实体商人争先恐后地在商店关闭期间开设网上商店，并减少了人流，即使在大流行期间允许其门保持打开状态也是如此。结果，截至12月28日，Shopify的市值今年几乎增长了两倍，达到约1400亿美元，比拥有58年历史的折扣零售连锁店Target的市值高出50％以上。
 Etsy，以小商人和艺术家出售手工制品而闻名的在线市场，也从这一转变中获得了可观的利润。上半年，许多在线购物者涌向Etsy，以购买小商户迅速生产的口罩，但此后也开始购买其他非口罩产品。到今年第三季度，口罩销售额占Etsy总销售额的11％，低于春季季度的14％。总体而言，Etsy的收入在2020年前9个月中增长了100％以上。
 但是，对于个体小商家而言，网上购物的成功并不能保证，正如Shopify和Etsy的近期兴起所暗示的那样。如果您是一家小型零售商，并且想在Shopify上建立在线店面以避免依赖Amazon或Walmart.com，那么您可能仍需要从其他技术巨头之一（即Google或Facebook）购买广告来吸引客户到您的数字门。
 Shopify知道这一点，并已开始着手打造自己的在线市场，以通过将已经拥有的包裹跟踪应用程序（以前称为Arrive）转变为具有更多购物重点的应用程序来帮助在线购物者发现Shopify商家。现在自然称为商店。至于Etsy，现在有超过360万商人，这意味着小卖家需要为销售而战，有时要大笔砍价才能发现广告。 
对于许多小型实体零售商而言，这种流行病消除了忽略电子商务是一个可行选择的想法。当然，当大流行最终“结束”时，我们行为的某些变化（无论是与消费有关还是其他方面的变化）肯定会恢复到2019年左右。受欢迎的餐馆和酒吧将再次吸引人群。旅行将回升，体育赛事和音乐会也将反弹。
 但是，我们购买商品的许多方式都会改变。在购买基本消费品（如牙膏，肥皂和卫生纸）时，亲自购买这些物品与在线购买相比几乎没有任何优势，除非您通过在Costco这样的仓库俱乐部进行批量购买节省了很多钱。
 在线购买其中一些低价商品的主要障碍是一些在线零售商要求的最低订购量，以使其在经济上可行才能发货。但是订阅Amazon Prime或Walmart +之类的运输会员资格可以消除最低运输限额，或者您可以执行我和我妻子经常在Target.com上所做的事情，并将商品保存在虚拟购物车中，直到我们需要足够的商品来超过阈值并下订单。
  对于在大流行期间首次尝试在线订购杂货的人来说，许多人将永远不会回到一致的店内杂货店购物，或者至少会更愿意偶尔在网上订购杂货店。恰当的例子：根据eMarketer的估算，到2020年底，巨型杂货连锁店Kroger有望首次跻身美国最大的在线零售商前十名。
 零售商Kroger在前十名中被梅西百货（Macy's）取代，这家曾经是百货商店的巨头已经在今年的全球健康危机之前陷入困境。这家传统零售商在1月份表示，计划关闭800多家门店中的125家。这场流行病是数家百货连锁店棺材中的最后钉子，对于像梅西百货这样并不处于困境但已经陷入困境的零售商来说，艰难的战斗更加激烈。长期恶化的中产阶级，缺乏商品差异化和冷漠的客户服务都在使客户对折扣连锁店和在线零售商的忠诚度降低方面发挥了作用。
 对于如今拥有超过五十万美国人的百货连锁店而言，情况将变得更糟。根据商业房地产研究公司Green Street Advisors的估计，到2021年底，所有购物中心百货商店中的一半以上将关闭。百货商店占购物中心每三平方英尺中的一处，因此购物中心行业也有可能陷入危机。
 那么，我们该何去何从？ 2020年加速的购物行为转变必将在许多方面使我们作为消费者的生活变得更轻松-这很重要。但是我们不仅作为消费者存在，我们的朋友，家人和邻居也没有。我们是依靠实体零售商为主要雇主的社区的成员，这些企业在大流行和衰退中幸存下来将被迫重塑自己。我们，我们的朋友，家人和邻居从事零售工作，以维持生计甚至稳定的职业，这在该行业中前景日益渺茫。零售业中越来越多的招聘发生在电子商务仓库内，那里的职位薪水可能会更高，但工作往往更加繁琐。 
尽管希望Shopify今年的成功可能会吸引中小型商人在亚马逊墙外开拓独立的电子商务期货，但有关2020年的真相仍然存在：亚马逊，零售业的破坏球，今年变得越来越强大，而不是 减。 而且，随着我们在网上推动更多购买活动，这家科技巨头的力量只会越来越大。  4月，Vox为读者提供了一种以财政捐助来支持我们的工作的方法-我们的回应使我们震惊。 今年，我们的创始贡献者提供了支持，帮助我们创建了数以百万计的项目，以帮助他们了解混乱的一年并确保家人的安全。 读者的支持有助于我们减少对广告的依赖，并使资源密集型工作免费供需要的每个人使用。 我们希望在今年年底之前为支持者增加2020个创始贡献者。 立即为Vox捐款，最低只需3美元，即可帮助我们实现目标。
note_en:
On March 4, I commuted into Vox Media’s New York City headquarters for what would end up being the final time during the godforsaken year of 2020. On the way in, I made a pit stop for some coffee, spending $3.89 on a bottle of Chameleon Cold-Brew, but left without handing over a card or cash on the way out. That’s because I had made that final purchase at a cashierless convenience store owned by a certain e-commerce giant: Amazon. Nine months later, that last stop looks like an appropriate harbinger of the major changes that would sweep across a big part of American life this year: how and where we shop.
 Over the next few weeks, my family, and millions of others in the US, began relying on Amazon and other shopping websites and apps in record numbers as the Covid-19 pandemic swept across the US. Masks, toilet paper, soap, and hand sanitizer were in high demand. But so were groceries, restaurant meals, puzzles, printers, and  even dumbbells.
 At times, government-mandated store closures in some parts of the country meant that if you needed to purchase something deemed nonessential, the only places to get it were “essential” big-box stores — or online. As a result,  Amazon and other retail giants like Target and Walmart reaped the rewards, while retail chains and small boutiques that sold apparel or other “nonessential” merchandise were forced to close their doors and turn customers away. Nearly 10,000 stores in the US have closed permanently this year alone.
 When we look back at 2020 in the business world, we’ll remember it as the year online shopping stopped being the future of retail and catapulted firmly into the present. This was the year that local governments forced us to give up in-store shopping for weeks or months, and then when we had an opportunity to return when stores reopened,  we mostly kept shopping online anyway.
 There’s a reason people who previously shunned online shopping for stores are now sticking with it: It’s typically much more convenient than browsing through rows of aisles to track down what you need. But the acceleration in online shopping this year — which otherwise would have taken several years to happen — will have profound consequences on the way millions of Americans work; the way corporate power is concentrated; and the way local communities are reconstructed to account for the decline of retail store chains like department stores and the malls they’ve long anchored.
  At the end of last year, only around 13 percent of retail purchases — excluding auto and gas sales — were made online,  according to Mastercard. By the end of 2020, that figure stands at around 20 percent, or $1 out of every $5. During normal recent years, when e-commerce growth rates averaged between 12 percent and 16 percent, that kind of jump would have taken several years to happen. But US e-commerce sales  will have grown more than 30 percent in 2020, and there’s no going back.
 Amazon, unsurprisingly, has been a huge winner of this dramatic shift, growing its US retail business an estimated 39 percent this year and increasing its market share to 39 percent of all online retail in the US, according to eMarketer. The e-commerce giant has posted record sales and profits,  even with early warehouse capacity issues and delivery delays, internal  labor battles, and  spending billions on Covid-19-related precautions.
 And it showed off its unparalleled strengths right up until the end of the holiday season. While many other retailers stopped promising deliveries in time for Christmas a week or two in advance, Amazon was guaranteeing same-day and next-day delivery of some online orders made as late as December 23, thanks in part to its huge warehouse coverage across the US and  the continued expansion of its own delivery network.
 But Amazon isn’t alone. Walmart and Target have had huge years too, posting modest market share gains in online retail. Part of their success has come from selling groceries and other in-demand goods on the web during the early months of the pandemic, coupled with curbside and in-store pickup offerings that were already in existence for online orders.
   Small businesses have also had to take their sales online. The scale of this shift is reflected in the success of tech platforms catering to small and mid-sized merchants that don’t want to rely on Amazon. Shopify, which sells e-commerce software tools to small and mid-sized merchants, has seen its revenue grow nearly 100 percent year over year in the first nine months of the year.
 The Canada-based software giant has benefited from small brick-and-mortar merchants scrambling to set up online shops amid store closures and reduced foot traffic even when their doors are allowed to remain open during the pandemic. As a result, Shopify’s market cap has nearly tripled this year to around $140 billion as of December 28, or more than 50 percent more than that of Target, the 58-year-old discount retail chain.
 Etsy, the online marketplace known for handmade goods sold by small merchants and artists, has also profited handsomely from the shift. Many online shoppers flocked to Etsy in the first half of the year to purchase masks that small merchants were producing at a rapid rate, but have since gone on to purchase other non-mask items too. By the third quarter of the year, mask sales accounted for 11 percent of Etsy’s gross sales, down from 14 percent in the spring quarter. In total, Etsy’s revenue increased more than 100 percent in the first nine months of 2020.
 For individual small merchants themselves, however, success online isn’t close to a guarantee, as the recent rise of Shopify and Etsy might suggest. If you’re a small retailer and want to set up an online storefront with Shopify to avoid relying on Amazon or Walmart.com, you likely still need to buy ads from one of the other tech giants — namely, Google or Facebook — to attract customers to your digital door.
 Shopify knows this, and has started to tiptoe its way toward creating its own online marketplace to help online shoppers discover Shopify merchants by transforming a package-tracking app it already owned, previously called Arrive, into an app with more of a shopping focus. It’s now called, naturally, Shop. As for Etsy, it is now home to more than 3.6 million merchants, meaning small sellers need to fight for sales,  sometimes paying a hefty cut for advertisements in order to be discovered.
 For many small brick-and-mortar retailers, the pandemic has vanquished the idea that ignoring e-commerce is a viable option. Sure, when the pandemic is finally “over,” some of the changes in our behavior — whether consumption-related or otherwise — will certainly revert to something like 2019. Popular restaurants and bars will once again attract crowds. Travel will rebound, as should live sporting events and concerts.
 But many of the changes in how we buy things will stick. When it comes to the purchase of essential consumables — think toothpaste, soap, and toilet paper — there’s little to no advantage gained from buying those items in person versus online, unless you are saving considerable money by making bulk purchases at a warehouse club like Costco.
 The main obstacle to purchasing some of these low-priced goods online is the order minimums some online retailers require to make it economically feasible for them to ship them out. But subscribing to shipping memberships like Amazon Prime or Walmart+ can eliminate shipping minimums, or you can do what my wife and I often do on Target.com and save items in our virtual shopping cart until we are in need of enough items to cross the threshold and place an order.
  For people who tried out ordering groceries online for the first time during the pandemic, many will never return to consistent in-store grocery shopping or, at a minimum, will be more willing to place the occasional grocery order online. Case in point: For the first time, the giant grocery chain Kroger is expected to crack the top 10 list of largest online retailers in the US by the end of 2020, according to eMarketer estimates.
 The retailer being replaced by Kroger in the top 10 is Macy’s, the one-time department store giant that was already floundering before this year’s global health crisis; the legacy retailer said in January that it was planning to close 125 of its 800-plus stores. The  pandemic was the final nail in the coffin of several department store chains and made the uphill battle much steeper for ones like Macy’s that were not in dire straits but were already struggling. A long-deteriorating middle class, lack of merchandise differentiation, and indifferent customer service all played a role in ceding customer loyalty to discount chains as well as online retailers.
 And things will get worse for the department store chain sector, which today employs more than a half-million Americans. More than half of all mall-based department stores are expected to close by the end of 2021, according to estimates by Green Street Advisors, a commercial property research firm. And with department stores accounting for one out of every three square feet in shopping malls, the mall industry is likely headed to a crisis too.
 So where do we go from here? The shift in our shopping behavior that 2020 accelerated will certainly make our lives as consumers easier in many ways — and that counts for something. But we don’t exist solely as consumers, nor do our friends, family, and neighbors. We are members of communities that count on physical retailers as major employers, businesses that will be forced to reinvent themselves after surviving a pandemic and a recession. We, our friends, family members, and neighbors work retail jobs to make ends meet or maybe even as stable careers, a dwindling prospect in the industry. More and more of the hirings in retail are happening inside e-commerce warehouses, where the roles might pay better but where the work is often more grueling.
 Despite the hope that Shopify’s success this year may bring to small and mid-sized merchants carving out independent e-commerce futures outside of Amazon’s walls, this truth about 2020 remains:  Amazon, the retail wrecking ball, has gotten more powerful this year, not less. And as we push more of our purchases online, the tech giant’s power will only grow.

This year, support from our founding contributors has helped us create projects that millions relied on to understand a year of chaos, and to keep their families safe. Support from our readers helps us rely less on advertising, and keep our resource-intensive work free for everyone who needs it. We want to add 2,020 more founding contributors to our supporter base by the end of the year. Help us reach our goal by making a contribution to Vox today,  from as little as $3.
title: eMarketer：美国电子商务销售额近年来增长了12％-16％，到2020年将增长30％以上，亚马逊的美国零售业务在2020年估计同比增长39％
title_en: eMarketer: US e-commerce sales, up 12%-16% in recent years, will have grown by 30%+ in 2020 and Amazon's US retail business grew an estimated 39% YoY in 2020

note:
2020年是全球移动应用和游戏支出创纪录的一年，11月单年首次超过1000亿美元。根据Sensor Tower Store Intelligence的初步估计，这一趋势在圣诞节持续了下来，当时全球消费者在Apple的App Store和Google Play上的花费估计为4.076亿美元。这个数字比2019年的约3.03亿美元增长了34.5％，比去年同期增长了17.7％，去年支出同比增长了17.7％。
 圣诞节的手机支出占当月迄今为止总支出的4.5％，从12月1日至12月27日，全球移动支出约为90亿美元。大部分的假日支出是手机游戏，从2019年圣诞节的2.324亿美元增长到27％。今年为2.956亿美元。腾讯的《王者荣耀》是该类别的领导者，其消费者支出约为1070万美元，比2019年同期的350万美元增长了205.7％。
  今年圣诞节，消费者在非游戏应用上的支出为1.12亿美元，比去年同期的7,050万美元增长了59％。与去年圣诞节相比，非游戏应用程序的总支出增加了4.2个百分点。
 在Apple App Store和Google平台上，在游戏以外产生最多收入的类别是娱乐。在App Store上，娱乐应用达到了1,930万美元，占所有非游戏支出的21.8％。在Google Play上，该类别产生了430万美元，占总收入的18.5％。
 在手机游戏之外，TikTok是消费者支出方面排名第一的应用程序，圣诞节期间全球创造了470万美元的收入。
 与往年一样，Apple的App Store占据了这两个平台之间的大部分支出。它看到了支出的68.4％，即2.786亿美元，同比增长35.2％。 Google Play的收入为1.29亿美元，同比增长超过33％。
  今年圣诞节，美国消费者在两家应用商店中花费了近1.3亿美元。这个数字比去年同期的9370万美元增长38.7％，超过全球增长6个百分点。 
与全球市场一样，手机游戏在美国创造了最大的收入，从2019年的近6900万美元增长到8720万美元，增长了26.4％。非游戏应用程序从圣诞节的2480万美元增长72.2％，至今年圣诞节的4270万美元，其中娱乐系统的收入为960万美元，占所有非游戏应用程序的22.5％。
 Roblox是美国排名第一的手机游戏，今年圣诞节消费者支出同比增长40.4％，从470万美元增长到660万美元。迪士尼+是消费支出最大的非游戏应用，在美国创造了260万美元的收入，比2019年的180万美元增长了44.4％。
  正如我们对今年移动市场里程碑的回顾所显示的那样，2020年消费者行为和支出发生了前所未有的变化。今年创造了新的纪录，两家应用商店共同创造了超过1000亿美元的消费者支出，并且一些轰动一时的应用跃居榜首。我们将在一月初对全年进行分析。

note_en:
2020 has been a record-setting year for worldwide spending on mobile apps and games, which passed $100 billion in a single year for the first time ever in November. This trend continued on Christmas, when consumers around the globe spent an estimated $407.6 million across Apple’s App Store and Google Play, according to preliminary Sensor Tower  Store Intelligence estimates. This figure represents 34.5 percent year-over-year growth from approximately $303 million in 2019, and is nearly 17 points higher than the growth experienced last year when spending grew 17.7 percent Y/Y.
 Mobile spending on Christmas comprised 4.5 percent of the month’s total spending so far, which reached approximately $9 billion globally from December 1 to December 27. The majority of the holiday spending was on mobile games, which climbed 27 percent from $232.4 million on Christmas 2019 to $295.6 million this year. Tencent’s  Honor of Kings was the category leader with approximately $10.7 million in consumer spending, up 205.7 percent Y/Y from $3.5 million on the same day in 2019.
  Consumers spent $112 million on non-game apps this Christmas, up 59 percent from $70.5 million in the previous year. Compared to last Christmas, non-game apps saw 4.2 percentage points more total spending.
 The category that generated the most revenue outside of games on both Apple’s App Store and Google’s platform was Entertainment. On the App Store, Entertainment apps reached $19.3 million or 21.8 percent of all non-game spending. On Google Play, the category generated $4.3 million or 18.5 percent of all revenue generated.
 Outside of mobile games,  TikTok was the top app in terms of consumer spending, generating $4.7 million globally in revenue on Christmas.
 As in previous years, Apple’s App Store captured the bulk of the spending between the two platforms. It saw 68.4 percent of the spending, or $278.6 million, up 35.2 percent Y/Y. Google Play saw $129 million in revenue, up more than 33 percent Y/Y.
  U.S. consumers spent nearly $130 million across both app stores this Christmas. This figure represents 38.7 percent Y/Y growth from $93.7 million last year, and exceeds global growth by 6 points.
  As on global marketplaces, mobile games generated the most revenue in the U.S., climbing 26.4 percent from nearly $69 million in 2019 to $87.2 million. Non-game apps grew 72.2 percent from $24.8 million to $42.7 million this Christmas, Entertainment leading with $9.6 million generated or 22.5 percent of all non-game spending.
 Roblox was the top mobile game in the U.S., with consumer spending climbing 40.4 percent Y/Y from $4.7 million to $6.6 million this Christmas.  Disney+ was the top non-game app for consumer spending, generating $2.6 million in the U.S., which was up 44.4 percent from $1.8 million in 2019.
  As shown in our retrospective on this year’s  mobile market milestones, 2020 saw an unprecedented shift in consumer behavior and spending. This year set new records, with both app stores collectively generating more than $100 billion in consumer spending and a number of blockbuster apps jumping to the top of the charts. We’ll have an analysis of the full year in early January.

纽约州金融服务部（Bitlicense）监管机构已授权GMO发行美元和日元挂钩的稳定币。后者“将是向公众提供的同类产品中的第一个。”迄今为止，监管机构已为从事加密活动的公司发放了27份章程和许可证。
  根据纽约银行法，纽约州金融服务部（DFS）已向日本巨型GMO Internet的子公司GMO-z.com Trust Company Inc.授予宪章。监管机构周二宣布，这使该公司能够作为有限责任信托公司运营，并详细说明：
 经DFS批准，GMO被授权在纽约发行，管理和赎回日元和与美元挂钩的稳定币。日元稳定币将是首个面向公众的币种。
 公告继续说：“包括授予GMO的宪章，迄今为止，DFS已经为从事虚拟货币业务活动的公司批准了27份宪章和许可证。”
 GMO-z Trust Company总裁Ken Nakamura表示：“我们正在采取行动，发行第一只受监管的日元挂钩稳定币，这在很多人看来都是避险资产。但是我们还是这个领域的开拓者和创新者，他们设想构建区块链技术的新应用，从而改变我们与传统金融服务的关系。”
  DFS于2015年开始为从事加密货币业务的公司颁发许可，此后修改了其加密货币监管方法。 “这包括通过自认证框架和硬币绿名单简化公司采用新硬币的过程，利用部门的有条件许可授权，并为申请人和被许可实体提供关于DFS流程和期望的新清晰度，”监管机构描述。

The New York State Department of Financial Services, the Bitlicense regulator, has authorized GMO to issue U.S. dollar and Japanese yen-pegged stablecoins. The latter “will be the first of its kind available to the public.” To date, the regulator has given out 27 charters and licenses for companies engaged in crypto activities.
  The New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS) has granted a charter under New York Banking Law to GMO-z.com Trust Company Inc., a subsidiary of the Japanese giant GMO Internet. This enables the company to operate as a limited liability trust company, the regulator announced Tuesday, elaborating:
 With DFS approval, GMO is authorized to issue, administer, and redeem Japanese yen and U.S. dollar-pegged stablecoins in New York. The Japanese yen stablecoin will be the first of its kind available to the public.
 “Including the charter granted to GMO, to date DFS has approved 27 charters and licenses for companies engaged in virtual currency business activity,” the announcement continues.
 GMO-z Trust Company president Ken Nakamura commented: “We’re breaking ground with our move to issue the first regulated JPY-pegged stablecoin, which many see as a safe haven asset. But we are also pioneers and innovators in this space who envision building new applications of blockchain technology that transform our relationship with traditional financial services.”
  The DFS began licensing firms engaged in cryptocurrency businesses in 2015 and has since  revised its  crypto regulatory approach. “This includes streamlining the process for companies to adopt new coins through a self-certification framework and a coins Greenlist, making use of the department’s conditional licensing authority, and providing new clarity about DFS processes and expectations to applicants and licensed entities,” the regulator described.

note:
由Edge提出的Game Changers是一个新的社论系列，从控制台战争的历史，到1994年最初的PlayStation发行，再到Xbox数十亿美元的红色救灾计划，更深入地探讨了关键时刻。每个插曲都会回顾当时的行业（背景），重播Edge杂志报道的关键时刻（时刻），与相关人员进行当今访谈（内幕故事），并考虑事件的历史影响（接下来发生了什么？）。新一集的“改变游戏规则”将于本周每天格林尼治标准时间下午5点/美国东部时间下午1点首次亮相。
 在过去的二十年中，微软已成为视频游戏行业最大的公司之一。现在，随着它准备推出两个新的Xbox，它已经开始质疑控制台范例本身。任天堂可能是定制硬件的主人，索尼是最明显的竞争对手，但是微软的游戏业务现在既是硬件又是跨党派软件。
  这似乎在某种程度上是不可避免的–毕竟，这家公司的财力比几乎任何其他公司都要高，因此，Xbox被视为强行进入市场。这当然是胡说八道：在视频游戏行业中，如果产品糟透了，预算就没有多大用处。
  在这里，我们回到Xbox的诞生之初，这个想法遭到了内部的反对，政治的批评，有时甚至是完全的嘲笑–也许在Microsoft的一家规模庞大的公司中不可避免，有如此多的利益相关者参与其中。但是，一支小的内部团队证明了自己的能力和决心，足以想象出一种新型的游戏机-索尼的PlayStation团队可能已经看到了这种游戏机，但是到那时，这并不重要。
 Edge详细记录了Xbox的早期历史，在这里我们重新审视了当时主要玩家所说的话。然后，我们与最初具有这个想法的人Seamus Blackley进行了交谈，讨论了使游戏机真正成为现实的斗争，以及为何微软联合创始人比尔·盖茨（Bill Gates）冒着很大的风险而冒险。
   究竟是什么导致Microsoft在2001年推出了游戏机？商业理由被隐藏在一百万封机密电子邮件中，但是我们知道市场已经成熟了。
  Xbox（早期称为＆＃34; X-Box＆＃34;和＆＃34; X-box＆＃34;）来自DirectX，它是Microsoft在PC游戏开发中常用的编程接口套件。 DirectX团队的四名成员– Kevin Bachus，Seamus Blackley，Ted Hase和Otto Berkes –跳上了Bill Gates＆＃39;建议PlayStation 2对Windows PC构成威胁，并且Microsoft需要可以竞争的产品。 
曾与Sony一起创建PS2系统软件，并为Sega的Dreamcast做过同样的事情，微软为控制台项目选择代号Midway是一个刻意的陈述，这似乎在一个更开明的时代，指的是第二次世界大战中美军击败日本的战斗。
 Xbox项目很快吸引了Microsoft的Windows软件架构师Nat Brown和游戏负责人Ed Fries，后者倡导在控制台中包含硬盘驱动器。人们认为，使用PC组件将消除软件开发人员在为控制台制作游戏方面的障碍，并使机器在功能和功能方面都与众不同。在线访问也将是一个大卖点，从而导致2002年推出Xbox Live。
 关于原始Xbox的一件事很明显，那就是它的大型控制器，绰号为Duke。该设备的设计是由于制造商Mitsumi Electric拒绝为微软制造两部分式堆叠电路板而产生的，因为它已经为Sony的DualShock 2制造了相同的东西。微软必须制造处理几乎三倍大的东西。公爵从未在日本推出过，在那里测试人员讨厌笨重的设计。较小的Controller S（使用更紧凑的板）与日式游戏机捆绑在一起，最终演变为我们今天所熟悉的Xbox控制器。
 关键时刻：Edge杂志在发布前对Xbox怎么说？
  深入了解微软总部，揭露Edge第88期（2000年9月），比尔·盖茨（Bill Gates）刚刚获得了X-Box的最终批准。像这样说，微软温和的董事长兼首席软件架构师（他于同年1月辞去首席执行官一职）听起来像是一个卡通小人，而不是有一天会帮助消灭小儿麻痹症的人。在西非。那和连字符不是唯一要更改的内容。
 ＆＃34;有史以来第一次，一生都在开发软件的人正在从头开始创建硬件。 Edge说，颇为虔诚。当时，微软声称是出于必要而参加了主机游戏竞赛，Seamus Blackley（X-Box高级技术团队的主管）表示。 ＆＃34;消费者最想要什么？好游戏。我们如何得到的？通过为开发人员提供他们所需的所有工具和硬件。开发人员和发布者告诉我们他们想要什么，并且[我们]在提供支持和见解方面处于独特的位置。从本质上讲，X-Box是一家大型软件公司，在这个不幸的硬件方面，我们必须学习如何处理。此后，微软大举购物，收购了22个游戏工作室以及Zenimax，这意味着如今的业务比布莱克利当时所能想象的要大。
 布莱克利（Blackley）在整个Edge报道中的语气有些令人惊讶，他的同事埃德·弗里斯（Ed Fries）（游戏发行副总裁）也处于类似的境地：世界上还有另一家公司可以做到吗？这个？我不这么认为，因为它需要很多不同的部分，尤其是软件。市场营销，开发人员支持，全球销售和分销...＆＃34; 
微软最初的计划是在2000年秋季推出Xbox，以便与PlayStation 2并驾齐驱。不过，比尔·盖茨还有其他想法，并且该游戏机将在同年推出，世嘉公司宣布其计划将第三方-只要。但是在那之前它已经进行了几年。 ＆＃34;它是在所有地方的飞机上降临的，布莱克利回忆道。 ＆＃34;我正在看年底的PC硬件，然后我开始思考，＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆nbsp;＆quot; ＃39;。＆＃34;
 ＆＃34;我们[意识到]可以生产类似控制台的东西，但是具有PC的所有优势，＆＃34;凯文·巴克斯（Kevin Bachus）解释说，他当时是微软的第三方总监。巴克斯（Bachus）和布莱克利（Blackley）于1999年1月将这个想法带到了弗里斯（Fries），并在不久后将J Allard任命为团队的总经理。 Allard刚从赛车上摔下来摔伤了脚踝，但无论如何他还是开始在Xbox OS上工作。该小组与盖茨和微软首席执行官史蒂夫·鲍尔默举行了为期三个月的会议。 ＆＃34;大多数过程都在确定X-Box不再是什么，＆＃34;炸薯条召回。
  控制台不是盖茨和微软最初的期望，但是它也颠覆了微软一直以来的工作方式。
 ＆＃34;小组每次决定将X-Box进一步专注于游戏，＆＃34; Edge写道，它面临着另一场公司之战。与PC游戏，生产力套件和网络电视兼容的想法被放弃了。但布莱克利说，最需要改变的是游戏开发过程本身。 ＆＃34;为什么每次新系统出炉时都会扔掉您所知道的一切？他问。从开发成本的角度和质量的角度来看，这毫无意义。
 ＆＃34;我们有机会，＆＃34;炸薯条说。 ＆＃34;您决定在没有[索尼或任天堂的历史]的情况下做类似的事情，您可以对其进行重新审视。我们有机会解决很多错误。＆＃34;
 盖茨和鲍尔默在2000年情人节那天对Xbox项目表示了赞许，但董事长仍然对PC兼容性不足感到不满意。当比尔第一次听说X-Box不会与PC兼容时，该杂志写了微软团队成员的话，他应该开始俯身在Allard的桌子上，说了些什么，[Expletive] ...让我明白了... [expletive ] ...它不会与PC兼容。尽管如此，阿拉德和船员还是不会让步。
 Xbox确实受益于PC零件的构建。该行业的竞争（微软曾被指控在Internet Explorer反托拉斯案中被扼杀）迫使芯片价格下跌，这意味着Xbox可能以消费者友好的价格进入市场。该控制台不是盖茨和微软最初期望的，但是它也颠覆了微软一直以来的工作方式。 ＆＃34;我们正在销售新产品概念和新剧本，阿拉德说，这是一种全新的商业模式。 
第88期让Edge陷入沉思状态，思考游戏行业的变化，从封面上自己对《机器人的崛起》的称赞（破坏者：它没有很好地评价）到索尼转向PS2的计划进入娱乐中心，直到PS3才能看到真正的成果。 ＆＃34;微软将像拉斯维加斯的豪赌者一样投入超过50亿美元，以努力为自己创造一个利基。谁能预见到某些世界上最受人谴责的软件的创建者现在准备提供动态，真正可行的[视频游戏]平台？
 ＆＃34; X-box具有PC传统的所有优点...但是PC没有任何缺点，＆＃34; Climax首席执行官卡尔·杰弗里（Karl Jeffrey）在同一期杂志中表示。如今，随着Microsoft和Sony都发布了基于PC架构的第二代游戏机来运行，我们很高兴与盖茨初次见面，因为他们的重要性是重新定义视频游戏机的基本本质。
 内幕消息：X-Box高级技术团队前总监Seamus Blackley回顾Xbox最早的几年
   ＆＃34;比尔·盖茨是一个承担巨大风险的人，他很有想象力。他是一位冒险家，他是一位知识分子冒险家，周围没有胡扯-他没有时间或宽容。
  Microsoft PR故事和发生的事情之间存在某种脱节。尤其是在我离开之后，事情发生了改写，在那儿，我只是一个更大团队的一员，而所有这一切。现实情况是，我有一个从波士顿乘飞机的想法，我向凯文[巴克斯]展示了，而他向他的老板展示了。然后我向奥托（伯克斯）和三个或四个家伙展示。所有其他这些人都曾在Microsoft工作，但我是新来的。因此，我就是那个坚持不懈地坚持下去的人。
 ＆＃34;我在那架飞机上写并向凯文展示的建议的基础是，使PC架构保持不变，但冻结，因此就像制造完全相同的PC一样。因此，您可以进行超优化，因为PC游戏和主机游戏之间的差异（尤其是当时）是PC可能具有谁知道什么声音功能，谁知道什么图形卡–也许根本没有图形卡。
 ＆＃34;因此，我们的想法是采用每个人都知道并感到满意的架构，然后冻结它，使其成为具有高性能和良好图形芯片的固定规格。这就是所有的魔力。它并不喜欢它必须比任何东西都要强大。 
＆＃34;但这是一个新概念。因为我们的客户是开发人员，而不是消费者。我们对开发人员的承诺是，他们可以在我们的平台上比PlayStation即时了解并提高生产力。就是这样这就是销售的关键所在，因为如果这是真的，那么您将获得最好的游戏，句号–这就是商业模式。
 ＆＃34;现在看来已成定局，但当时还不是。这是历史的偏见。当我们回顾历史时，我们经常认为当时的参与人员有一些宏伟的计划，并对即将发生的事情有清晰的认识，当然那根本不是真的。 [微软]是一家大公司，政治众多，人们的议程各不相同，有人说，是的，我们现在将支持Xbox。因为他们实际上想要其他东西，或者是试图颠覆它的人，但是当你面对面时告诉你他们在支持你，以及所有这些东西。
 ＆＃34;对我来说，在一个小会议室里和比尔·盖茨和史蒂夫·鲍尔默开会。而且史蒂夫从来没有，我不认为，曾经认为这是一个好主意。我有一个故事告诉我我在餐厅和凯文在一起的地方，史蒂夫·鲍尔默（Steve Ballmer）悄悄地躲在我们后面并大喊大叫，让我丢下食物：＆quot; YOU＆＃39 ;要失去我们所有的钱！＆＃39;
  本文介绍了一切的开始，但是二十年后的Xbox部门现在在哪里？ Xbox Series X In Detail涵盖了您需要了解的有关Microsoft全新的下一代控制台的所有信息。
 ＆＃34;但是，在这个小型会议室中，经过数月和数月的会议之后，比尔基本上告诉史蒂夫，尽管他对此感到非常沮丧，但我们只是这样做了，那一刻我以为那是真的发生。
 ＆＃34; [人们]对Bill的看法很奇怪。首先，比尔是一个承担巨大风险的人，并且他的想象力很高。这就是为什么他在世界范围内拥有这些庞大的项目，对吗？他是一个冒险家，他是一个知识分子冒险家，周围没有胡扯-他没有时间或宽容。在所有事情中，让我认为我们有机会的线索永远只是比尔所要求的诚实。因为我们诚实地不知道如何制作游戏机，但是我们确实了解游戏，并且我们确实相信市场机会。老实说，我们确实相信微软是当时唯一可以与索尼竞争的公司，这是事实。因为我们诚实地相信了这些事情，所以我们可以进行介绍，而废话内容为零。
 您必须坐在与比尔的会谈中，他的情况令人震惊。人们乱扔“天才”这个词一直以来，人们说“天才”的方式如果您遇到了一个天才的人，那是不正确的–当您遇到他们时，那个人似乎在外星人耳边窃窃私语，因为否则他们将无法知道这些事情。这是另一回事，比尔就是这样。 
因此，当人们做演讲时，比尔可以闻到胡说八道，他可以看到技术的问题，并提出可以减轻人们眼泪的问题。而且我们没有这种恐惧。太棒了。比尔很有想像力，而且很有冒险精神，这就是为什么他喜欢这个提议。出于这个原因，我认为我们打造Xbox之类的精神对他真的很有吸引力。
 （有传言说Xbox团队在某种程度上误导了盖茨，暗示一段时间该控制台将采用Microsoft的Windows操作系统。）
 ＆＃34;我们绝对有意蒙蔽了他。我绝对明确地多次写信给他说，我们当时正在这样做。您知道，这是很大的风险。如果成功的话，他会很高兴；如果不成功，那就不会那么快乐。但是请注意，现实是，您再次需要记住，这是一家不了解游戏的公司，更不用说游戏机了。这家公司的全部价值都基于Windows操作系统-他们将计算机的价值视为操作系统。好的，现在，如果您是该公司的内部员工，并且想要推销新平台的价值，那么您使用什么论点？您使用操作系统。
 现在，这并不意味着随着他们在项目进行过程中对它的更多思考，他们就不会明白这是不正确的，这就是发生了的事情。 Windows确实不再是此方面的价值主张。他们以前曾想过：“哦，我们应该建立一个Windows游戏平台-Windows将提供所有这些价值...”哪个是垃圾，对不对？这是垃圾，这是不正确的。没有哪位游戏玩家对Windows功能一事无成。但是对于雷德蒙德的家伙来说，这是真的。而且您不会说服他们。我的意思是，您是否试图说服您的祖父？
  ＆＃34;该计划成功的原因是孙子。我们攻击了他们弱小的敌人。而且我们没有在他们强大的地方攻击他们。＆＃34;
 ＆＃34;因此，您只需要稍作片刻冲浪，就可以放下自己的自豪感并保持冷静，直到每个人都学到足够多的东西，使他们明白您的论点。凯文（Kevin）过去常把它称为绝地思维技巧。最终，有人会向您提出您的想法–他们会说，“嘿，您知道，这根本不需要Windows。”而且，您必须控制自己想杀死他们的冲动，因为您已经告诉他们两年了，现在他们终于按照自己的想法提出了。但这就是绝地的头脑把戏：这些不是我们正在寻找的机器人。好的，这不是我们需要的操作系统。喝彩[鼓掌]。您是个天才，谢谢您的考虑。我们从未考虑过–我们认为它应该是Windows。
 ＆＃34;我很少进行竞争分析。这让我感到非常不安全，因为Sony的开发人员组织（开发人员受支持的方式）非常好。索尼是一家大型的消费电子公司，知道如何做到这一点。我们的价值主张是不要在任何这些地方直接与他们竞争。 
＆＃34;该计划成功的原因是孙子。我们攻击了他们弱小的敌人。我们没有在他们强大的地方攻击他们。我们的价值主张不是在PlayStation之外超越PlayStation；我们的价值主张是从他们未曾看到的方向脱颖而出。因为每个PlayStation开发套件都可以从PC上使用，对吗？我们的攻击是：嘿，这是开发人员实际上知道如何使用的盒子–我们将把它放入控制台，并且我们喜欢将电线剪断到开发工具包中拥有并使其成为控制台。那就是攻击。
 这样就不需要大量研究他们在做什么。这就需要研究如何使PC架构成为可负担得起的制造控制台。这根本不是要弄清楚索尼是如何做到的，而是要弄清楚新事物，这就是如果您真的想动摇它，那么您将如何进入市场，这就是我们所做的。
 我认为Xbox的成功归结于……你知道，我会充满诗意的。这是一个大公司内部实际玩家的非常纯粹的激情驱动的项目，它有能力实现这一目标。但正是这种愿景的纯粹性吸引了从开发人员到游戏玩家的每个人。它一直持续到今天。
 我为我们在Xbox上所做的所有这些工作以及所有从事此工作的人感到非常自豪，比尔（Bill）承担了风险，史蒂夫（Steve）克服了他的担忧，对此我感到非常自豪。所有这些人。我为他们和他们感到骄傲，并感到惊讶。但是Xbox社区的热情，我的意思是，它与其他游戏社区不同，与其他游戏迷不同-有一种想法，例如，对超纯的诚实热爱以某种方式进行游戏。那是它的核心，它始于那架飞机飞行，并在我们内部进行的所有其他对话中继续进行。这就是它起作用的原因，因为我们让那火焰一直活着。
